name: Danny Alvin
abstract: Danny Alvin (November 29, 1902 in New York City – December 6, 1958 in Chicago) was an American jazz drummer and bandleader. Alvin was the father of guitarist Teddy Walters.

about_quotes.speaker: Sol Yaged
about_quotes.evidence: I had a great drummer with me... his name was Danny Alvin... And we had a trio.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sol Yaged interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Art Hodes
about_quotes.evidence: Art Hodes and his trio have opened at Jimmy Ryan's at 52nd Street. And he's featuring Danny Alvin and Mezzrow

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Danny Alvin, Sid Catlett, drums... The session was played by forming three orchestras with those musicians

about_quotes.speaker: Roswell Rudd
about_quotes.evidence: I remember who they were very distinctly... and Danny Alvin on the drums, real funky sort of I guess you’d call it ethnic drummer from Chicago.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roswell Rudd interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Georg Brunis
about_quotes.evidence: Then I went out to the TomTom with Danny Alvin for three months.
